The archery contest drew twice the usual crowd. Turns out the new arrivals have some real talent.
The musicians argued about which songs to play until someone just started playing and the rest gave in.
The bonfire was larger than last year. Someone contributed an entire cart of dried timber and would not say where it came from.
The cooking contest ended in a draw because the judges could not agree. Both winners walked away satisfied, so it worked out.
Decorations went up three days early because the volunteers got overenthusiastic. By the actual day, half of them had blown down.
A wrestling match between two of the guards drew the biggest crowd of the evening. Neither won cleanly and both claimed victory.
The children organized their own parade without any adult help. It wound through every alley in the settlement before ending at the well.
Someone brought a cask of something strong from outside the settlement. By evening, half the council was singing and the other half pretended not to notice.
The lantern display along the main road was the best part. Hundreds of them, all different colors, hung from every post and railing.
The storyteller held the crowd for over an hour. Nobody moved, not even the little ones. That does not happen often.
There was a dispute over who would light the ceremonial fire. Two elders both claimed the right and neither would yield until a compromise was brokered.
The merchants set their prices too high on the first day and had to drop them by the second when nobody was buying.
A toast was raised to the founders. Most people joined in. A few stayed silent, which was noticed.
The dancing went well past midnight. The watch gave up trying to enforce quiet hours and joined in instead.
Preparations took a full week longer than planned because the committee kept changing the schedule.
The best moment was unplanned. A group of strangers passing through stopped to watch and ended up performing a song nobody had heard before.
By the end of the third day, everyone was exhausted but reluctant to admit the celebration was over. The cleanup took longer than the event itself.
